sprint: Page Background Colour -- a real per-page override, winning over the World's own wall tone
task: Real, confirmed bug: 'the creator background color. its available in right panel but changing it has not effect on the center pane slide.' Root cause: Context Panel's Background Colour swatch wrote to the Story Theme's global colours.frame sub-option, but the renderer's background-fill line has always unconditionally preferred the active World's own resolved wall tone over the Story Theme's frame colour whenever any World with a Frame Variation is active -- the common case today -- so the swatch's write never had priority to paint anything. It was also a GLOBAL write (every page at once), not scoped to the one page the user was editing. Fixed with a genuine per-page override (slide.metadata.cardOverrides.background, the same pattern every other Story-Author control in this codebase already uses) that now resolves first, ahead of both wall tone and the Story Theme default; Context Panel now reads/writes the current slide and calls the standard redraw/markDirty dispatch instead of the global Theme API. Verified via a git-stash A/B test against a real Frame-Variation-driven wall tone: before the fix the canvas pixel was completely unchanged after picking a new colour; after, it repaints immediately and is confirmed stored per-slide. Full regression across the existing 36-script suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors.
